title: quercus cocciferoides var. taliensis
intro: Quercus cocciferoides var. taliensis is a variant of the oak tree species, specifically a type of evergreen tree native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. Traditionally, Quercus cocciferoides var. taliensis is used in herbal preparations, often combined with other plants, to create teas, decoctions, and infusions that are used to treat various ailments.
intro_study: According to "Saudi journal of biological sciences", Quercus cocciferoides var. taliensis has antidiabetic properties, specifically reducing blood glucose levels in diabetic mice when treated with a methanolic extract.
